qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Code injection via unescaped const value in mock generation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies code injection via the unescaped `const` field in `getMockScalar()` during mock generation (packages/mock/src/faker/getters/scalar.ts:261). The advisory describes code injection via `x-enumDescriptions` in `getEnumImplementation()` during enum generation in @orval/core. These are different input fields (const vs x-enumDescriptions), different vulnerable functions (getMockScalar vs getEnumImplementation), different code paths (mock generation vs enum generation), and different primary packages (@orval/mock vs @orval/core). While both share the same broad vulnerability class (code injection via unsanitized spec values in orval), the finding does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes code injection via unsanitized x-enumDescriptions in getEnumImplementation() during enum generation in @orval/core. The sole qualifying finding identifies a distinct code injection via the const field in getMockScalar() during mock generation, which is a different function, different input field, and different code path.
evidence: Advisory root cause: x-enumDescriptions values embedded without escaping in getEnumImplementation() in @orval/core during const enum generation
Finding code-injection-via-unescaped-pattern: const values interpolated without escaping in getMockScalar() in packages/mock/src/faker/getters/scalar.ts:261 during mock generation
